result.summary: privateca.googleapis.com exposes a Google Cloud Certificate Authority REST API and is also operated via the `gcloud privateca` CLI; both require Google Cloud credentials/OAuth access tokens.
result.description: Google Cloud Certificate Authority Service manages private certificate authorities and certificate issuance. It lets organizations create CA pools, manage certificate authorities, issue certificates, and related revocation resources.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token (ADC or gcloud user/service account credentials)
gcp_oauth.setup: For local development, install the [Google Cloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) and run `gcloud auth application-default login` to create Application Default Credentials, or `gcloud auth login` for user CLI credentials. For server-to-server use, create and attach a service account in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and grant it the needed IAM roles for Certificate Authority Service; Google client libraries and ADC will use those credentials automatically. The CA Service auth guide says you can authenticate using `gcloud` CLI credentials or Application Default Credentials (ADC).

variables.name: project
variables.description: Google Cloud project ID in resource paths such as `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.

variables.name: location
variables.description: Google Cloud region/location in resource paths.

surfaces.name: gcloud privateca CLI
surfaces.notes: The Google Cloud CLI includes `gcloud privateca ...` commands for Certificate Authority Service operations and authenticates with the user's gcloud credentials.
